title: Fresh Crispy Dutch Toor Dal
description: A post-workout dish featuring toor dal with a crispy exterior and fresh flavors, suitable for those who don't enjoy onions or garlic. This recipe is a twist on a traditional Dutch dish, combining the health benefits of toor dal with the convenience of a one-pan meal.
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the coconut oil over medium-high heat.
Add the toor dal and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until it is lightly browned.
Add the chicken broth, ginger powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the toor dal is tender.
Add the lemon juice and cayenne pepper to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Using a potato masher or the back of a spoon, mash some of the toor dal to create a chunky texture.
In a small bowl, mix together the water and cornstarch. Add the mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Continue to simmer the mixture for 5 minutes, or until it has thickened and the toor dal is coated in a crispy, golden-brown crust.
Serve the Fresh Crispy Dutch Toor Dal hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Note: You can also make this recipe in a slow cooker. Simply brown the toor dal in a skillet, then transfer it to the slow cooker and cook on low for 6-8 hours, stirring occasionally.
Serve the toor dal hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
